claude-oracle.yml:
name: 🔮 Gemini↔Claude Oracle

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      pr_number:
        description: 'PR number to review'
        required: true
        type: number

jobs:
  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # Job 1: Claude Review Pass
  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  claude-review:
    name: 🤖 Claude Review Pass
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      pull-requests: write
    outputs:
      review_file: /tmp/claude-review.md

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Build full diff
        run: |
          if [ "${{ github.event_name }}" = "pull_request" ]; then
            git diff origin/${{ github.base_ref }}...HEAD > /tmp/full-diff.txt
          else
            git diff HEAD~1 HEAD > /tmp/full-diff.txt
          fi

      - name: Claude first-pass review
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        with:
          anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
          direct_prompt: |
            You are conducting the FIRST PASS review for the Jourwigo Gemini↔Claude Oracle.

            ## Memory (read first)
            - .github/agents/memory/wherigo-protocol.yml
            - .github/agents/memory/gemini-integration.yml
            - .github/agents/memory/android-conventions.yml
            - .github/agents/memory/false-positives.yml

            ## Task
            Read /tmp/full-diff.txt and conduct a comprehensive code review.

            Review every aspect:
            - Security (secrets, injection, permissions)
            - Correctness (logic bugs, edge cases)
            - Performance (unnecessary re-renders, blocking calls, memory leaks)
            - Wherigo domain correctness
            - Test coverage

            Write your findings to /tmp/claude-review.md in this EXACT format
            (use these exact section headers so the synthesis agent can parse them):

            ```markdown
            # Claude Code Review

            ## CRITICAL_ISSUES
            <!-- List each as: ITEM: <description> | FILE: <file> | LINE: <line or range> -->

            ## WARNINGS
            <!-- List each as: ITEM: <description> | FILE: <file> -->

            ## POSITIVE_OBSERVATIONS
            <!-- List each as: ITEM: <description> -->

            ## CONFIDENCE_SCORE
            <!-- Single number 0-100 representing your confidence in this review -->
            ```

            IMPORTANT: Do NOT post a PR comment in this job. Only write to /tmp/claude-review.md.
            The synthesis job will post the final combined comment.

      - name: Upload Claude review artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: claude-review
          path: /tmp/claude-review.md
          retention-days: 1

  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # Job 2: Gemini Review Pass
  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  gemini-review:
    name: ✨ Gemini Review Pass
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
    outputs:
      review_file: /tmp/gemini-review.md

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Set up Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Build full diff
        run: |
          if [ "${{ github.event_name }}" = "pull_request" ]; then
            git diff origin/${{ github.base_ref }}...HEAD > /tmp/full-diff.txt
          else
            git diff HEAD~1 HEAD > /tmp/full-diff.txt
          fi

      - name: Run Gemini review via Node script
        env:
          GEMINI_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.GEMINI_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          node - << 'NODESCRIPT'
          const { GoogleGenAI } = require("@google/genai");
          const fs = require("fs");

          const ai = new GoogleGenAI({ apiKey: process.env.GEMINI_API_KEY });

          async function main() {
            const diff = fs.readFileSync("/tmp/full-diff.txt", "utf8");

            // Read memory files for context
            let memory = "";
            const memoryFiles = [
              ".github/agents/memory/gemini-integration.yml",
              ".github/agents/memory/wherigo-protocol.yml",
              ".github/agents/memory/false-positives.yml",
            ];
            for (const f of memoryFiles) {
              try {
                memory += `\n\n## ${f}\n${fs.readFileSync(f, "utf8")}`;
              } catch {}
            }

            const prompt = `You are conducting a code review for the Jourwigo Android project.
          This is a Wherigo geocaching app with a React/TypeScript frontend and Android native layer.

          ## Domain Memory
          ${memory}

          ## Code Diff to Review
          \`\`\`diff
          ${diff.slice(0, 50000)}
          \`\`\`

          Conduct a comprehensive review focusing on:
          - Correctness and logic bugs
          - Security issues (especially around Gemini API key handling and prompt injection)
          - Performance concerns
          - Wherigo/geocaching domain correctness
          - React/TypeScript best practices

          Respond in EXACTLY this format (use these exact section headers):

          # Gemini Code Review

          ## CRITICAL_ISSUES
          <!-- List each as: ITEM: <description> | FILE: <file> | LINE: <line or range> -->

          ## WARNINGS
          <!-- List each as: ITEM: <description> | FILE: <file> -->

          ## POSITIVE_OBSERVATIONS
          <!-- List each as: ITEM: <description> -->

          ## CONFIDENCE_SCORE
          <!-- Single number 0-100 -->`;

            try {
              const response = await ai.models.generateContent({
                model: "gemini-2.5-pro",
                contents: [{ role: "user", parts: [{ text: prompt }] }],
              });
              fs.writeFileSync("/tmp/gemini-review.md", response.text || "# Gemini Code Review\n\n## CRITICAL_ISSUES\n\n## WARNINGS\n\n## POSITIVE_OBSERVATIONS\n\n## CONFIDENCE_SCORE\n50");
              console.log("Gemini review written.");
            } catch (err) {
              console.error("Gemini review failed:", err.message);
              fs.writeFileSync("/tmp/gemini-review.md", `# Gemini Code Review\n\n_Review failed: ${err.message}_\n\n## CRITICAL_ISSUES\n\n## WARNINGS\n\n## POSITIVE_OBSERVATIONS\n\n## CONFIDENCE_SCORE\n0`);
            }
          }

          main();
          NODESCRIPT

      - name: Upload Gemini review artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: gemini-review
          path: /tmp/gemini-review.md
          retention-days: 1

  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # Job 3: Meta-Synthesis – Claude reads both reviews and posts final verdict
  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  oracle-synthesis:
    name: 🔮 Oracle Synthesis
    needs: [claude-review, gemini-review]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      pull-requests: write

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Download both review artifacts
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          path: /tmp/reviews/

      - name: Locate review files
        run: |
          find /tmp/reviews/ -name "*.md" -exec echo {} \; -exec wc -l {} \;
          cp /tmp/reviews/claude-review/claude-review.md /tmp/claude-review.md 2>/dev/null || echo "# Claude Code Review\n\n_Not available_" > /tmp/claude-review.md
          cp /tmp/reviews/gemini-review/gemini-review.md /tmp/gemini-review.md 2>/dev/null || echo "# Gemini Code Review\n\n_Not available_" > /tmp/gemini-review.md

      - name: Run Oracle Synthesis Claude Agent
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        with:
          anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
          direct_prompt: |
            You are the Oracle – the synthesis meta-agent for Jourwigo's multi-model review system.

            You have received two independent code reviews of the same PR:
            - Claude's review: /tmp/claude-review.md
            - Gemini's review: /tmp/gemini-review.md

            Read both reviews carefully.

            ## Your Synthesis Tasks

            ### 1. Find Agreements
            Issues flagged by BOTH Claude and Gemini are high-confidence findings.
            List them under "## ✅ Agreed Findings".

            ### 2. Find Disputes
            Issues flagged by ONE reviewer but NOT the other are disputed.
            These deserve human attention – they may be false positives OR one model missed something.
            List them under "## ⚡ Disputed Findings".

            ### 3. Exclusive Findings
            Issues only Claude found → "## 🤖 Claude-Only Findings"
            Issues only Gemini found → "## ✨ Gemini-Only Findings"

            ### 4. Positive Observations (combined)
            Merge positive observations from both reviews.

            ### 5. Verdict
            Based on the combined analysis:
            - **APPROVE**: No critical issues, only minor warnings
            - **REQUEST_CHANGES**: Critical issues found (agreed or high-confidence)
            - **COMMENT**: Warnings/suggestions only

            ### 6. Post the Oracle comment to the PR
            ```
            gh pr comment ${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.event.inputs.pr_number }} \
              --body "## 🔮 Oracle Review (Claude × Gemini)

            > _Two AI models reviewed this PR independently. Agreements are high-confidence; disputes need human judgment._

            ### Verdict: <APPROVE / REQUEST_CHANGES / COMMENT>

            ---

            ## ✅ Agreed Findings
            _(Both Claude and Gemini flagged these – high confidence)_

            <agreed critical issues and warnings>

            ---

            ## ⚡ Disputed Findings
            _(One model flagged, one didn't – needs human judgment)_

            | Finding | Claude | Gemini |
            |---------|--------|--------|
            | <issue> | 🚩 Flagged | ✅ No issue |

            ---

            ## 🤖 Claude-Only Findings
            <issues only Claude raised>

            ## ✨ Gemini-Only Findings
            <issues only Gemini raised>

            ---

            ## 🌟 Positive Observations
            <combined positives>

            ---

            | Metric | Claude | Gemini |
            |--------|--------|--------|
            | Confidence Score | <score>/100 | <score>/100 |
            | Critical Issues | <N> | <N> |
            | Warnings | <N> | <N> |

            ---
            _Oracle synthesis by Claude Code | Gemini review by Gemini 2.5 Pro | [memory](.github/agents/memory/)_"
            ```

            ### 7. Update memory with new patterns
            If this synthesis revealed a new recurring pattern (something both models agree on
            that isn't already in the memory files), append it to the appropriate memory file.
claude-orchestrate.yml:
name: 🧠 Claude Orchestrator

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]
  push:
    branches: [main, master, develop]

jobs:
  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # Job 1: Classify changed files (fast, shell-only)
  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  classify:
    name: Classify changes
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      pull-requests: read
    outputs:
      run_android: ${{ steps.detect.outputs.run_android }}
      run_frontend: ${{ steps.detect.outputs.run_frontend }}
      run_services: ${{ steps.detect.outputs.run_services }}
      run_gradle: ${{ steps.detect.outputs.run_gradle }}
      run_docs: ${{ steps.detect.outputs.run_docs }}
      changed_files: ${{ steps.detect.outputs.changed_files }}

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Detect changed file categories
        id: detect
        run: |
          if [ "${{ github.event_name }}" = "pull_request" ]; then
            FILES=$(git diff --name-only origin/${{ github.base_ref }}...HEAD)
          else
            FILES=$(git diff --name-only HEAD~1 HEAD 2>/dev/null || git diff --name-only HEAD)
          fi

          echo "Changed files:"
          echo "$FILES"
          echo "changed_files<<EOF" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "$FILES" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "EOF" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

          # Android: anything in app/ directory or Java/Kotlin files
          if echo "$FILES" | grep -qE '^app/|\.kt$|\.java$|AndroidManifest\.xml'; then
            echo "run_android=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "run_android=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

          # Frontend: src/ directory, React/TS/CSS files
          if echo "$FILES" | grep -qE '^src/|\.tsx?$|\.css$|vite\.config|tsconfig'; then
            echo "run_frontend=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "run_frontend=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

          # Services: specifically the AI service layer
          if echo "$FILES" | grep -qE '^src/services/|aiService'; then
            echo "run_services=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "run_services=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

          # Gradle/deps: build files, gradle, package.json
          if echo "$FILES" | grep -qE '\.gradle$|gradle-wrapper|package\.json|package-lock\.json|settings\.gradle'; then
            echo "run_gradle=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "run_gradle=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

          # Docs: markdown, readme, metadata
          if echo "$FILES" | grep -qE '\.md$|README|metadata\.json|\.txt$'; then
            echo "run_docs=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "run_docs=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # Job 2: Android Expert Review
  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  android-review:
    name: 🤖 Android Expert
    needs: classify
    if: needs.classify.outputs.run_android == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      pull-requests: write

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Set up JDK 17
        uses: actions/setup-java@v4
        with:
          java-version: '17'
          distribution: 'temurin'

      - name: Build diff
        run: |
          if [ "${{ github.event_name }}" = "pull_request" ]; then
            git diff origin/${{ github.base_ref }}...HEAD -- 'app/**' '*.gradle' 'AndroidManifest.xml' > /tmp/android-diff.txt
          else
            git diff HEAD~1 HEAD -- 'app/**' '*.gradle' 'AndroidManifest.xml' > /tmp/android-diff.txt
          fi

      - name: Run Android Expert Claude Agent
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        with:
          anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
          direct_prompt: |
            You are the Android Expert for Jourwigo – a native Android Wherigo geocaching plugin.

            ## Memory (read first)
            - .github/agents/memory/wherigo-protocol.yml  – Wherigo/c:geo domain knowledge
            - .github/agents/memory/android-conventions.yml – project Android conventions
            - .github/agents/memory/false-positives.yml – known false positives to ignore

            ## Context
            Changed files in this push:
            ${{ needs.classify.outputs.changed_files }}

            The git diff for Android files is at /tmp/android-diff.txt.
            Read it thoroughly. Also read the affected source files in full context from the workspace.

            ## Your Review Tasks
            1. **Android Best Practices**: Check for thread violations, deprecated API usage,
               memory leaks (Context references in static fields, unregistered listeners), etc.
            2. **Wherigo Protocol Compliance**: Ensure c:geo plugin interface is respected.
               Check metadata.json still declares only the `geolocation` permission.
               Verify no new permissions were added to AndroidManifest.xml unnecessarily.
            3. **Privacy & Location Data**: Flag any Log.d/Log.v calls that print GPS coordinates
               or user location. Ensure location data is not persisted beyond session.
            4. **WebView Security**: If WebView code is changed, verify:
               - setAllowFileAccess(false)
               - setJavaScriptEnabled only when necessary
               - No addJavascriptInterface without @JavascriptInterface annotation
            5. **Test Coverage**: For every new public method/class added, check if a test
               exists in app/src/test/ or app/src/androidTest/. If not, note it.

            ## Output
            If this is a PR (${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && 'yes' || 'no' }}):
            Post a structured review comment via:
            ```
            gh pr comment ${{ github.event.pull_request.number || '0' }} --body "<your review>"
            ```

            Format your comment as:
            ```markdown
            ## 🤖 Android Expert Review

            ### Summary
            <one paragraph overview>

            ### 🔴 Critical Issues
            <issues that must be fixed – or "None">

            ### 🟡 Warnings
            <things to investigate – or "None">

            ### 🟢 Looks Good
            <what was done well>

            ### 🧪 Missing Tests
            <list of new code paths without test coverage – or "None detected">

            ---
            _Review by Claude Android Expert | [memory](.github/agents/memory/)_
            ```

            Write a brief summary to /tmp/android-review-status.txt:
            - "CRITICAL" if there are critical issues
            - "WARNINGS" if there are only warnings
            - "CLEAN" if all good

  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # Job 3: Frontend + Gemini Auditor
  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  frontend-audit:
    name: ⚛️ Frontend + Gemini Auditor
    needs: classify
    if: needs.classify.outputs.run_frontend == 'true' || needs.classify.outputs.run_services == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      pull-requests: write

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Set up Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Run TypeScript lint
        id: tslint
        run: npm run lint 2>&1 | tee /tmp/ts-lint-output.txt || true

      - name: Build diff
        run: |
          if [ "${{ github.event_name }}" = "pull_request" ]; then
            git diff origin/${{ github.base_ref }}...HEAD -- 'src/**' '*.tsx' '*.ts' '*.css' > /tmp/frontend-diff.txt
          else
            git diff HEAD~1 HEAD -- 'src/**' '*.tsx' '*.ts' '*.css' > /tmp/frontend-diff.txt
          fi

      - name: Run Frontend Audit Claude Agent
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        with:
          anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
          direct_prompt: |
            You are the Frontend & Gemini Integration Auditor for Jourwigo.

            ## Memory (read first)
            - .github/agents/memory/gemini-integration.yml – Gemini API patterns and security rules
            - .github/agents/memory/false-positives.yml – known false positives

            ## Context
            Changed files: ${{ needs.classify.outputs.changed_files }}
            Frontend diff: /tmp/frontend-diff.txt
            TypeScript lint output: /tmp/ts-lint-output.txt

            Stack: React 19, react-router-dom v7, Tailwind CSS v4, tailwind-merge + clsx,
            motion (Framer Motion), TypeScript ~5.8, Vite 6, @google/genai SDK.

            ## Your Audit Tasks

            ### 🔐 Security (Gemini Integration)
            1. Verify GEMINI_API_KEY is NEVER hardcoded. Only via process.env.GEMINI_API_KEY.
            2. Review all prompt strings in src/services/aiService.ts for injection vectors.
               User-supplied strings going directly into Gemini prompts without sanitization
               are a risk. Flag but don't block on minor cases.
            3. Check settings.enabled AND specific permission checks before every AI call.
            4. Verify no Gemini API calls happen in browser-executed code (only server-side).

            ### ⚛️ React / TypeScript Quality
            1. React 19 patterns: check for deprecated APIs (legacy context, string refs, etc.)
            2. react-router-dom v7: verify Route components use the v7 API (no Switch, no exact prop)
            3. TypeScript: flag `any` type usage added in this diff. Check for missing return types
               on exported functions.
            4. Check for missing keys in .map() calls.

            ### 🎨 Tailwind / Motion
            1. Tailwind v4 uses CSS-first config – flag any JS tailwind.config.js usage.
            2. Verify tailwind-merge (tm()) and clsx are used for conditional classes.
            3. Motion animations: flag any animation without a `prefers-reduced-motion` guard
               if it's a significant visual transition.

            ### 📝 TypeScript Lint
            Review /tmp/ts-lint-output.txt. Summarize any new errors introduced by this diff.

            ## Output
            If this is a PR (${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && 'yes' || 'no' }}):
            Post via:
            ```
            gh pr comment ${{ github.event.pull_request.number || '0' }} --body "<your review>"
            ```

            Format:
            ```markdown
            ## ⚛️ Frontend & Gemini Audit

            ### Summary
            <one paragraph>

            ### 🔐 Security Findings
            <or "None">

            ### ⚛️ React/TypeScript Issues
            <or "None">

            ### 🎨 Style & Animation
            <or "None">

            ### 📝 Lint Summary
            <summary of lint output>

            ---
            _Audit by Claude Frontend Expert | [memory](.github/agents/memory/)_
            ```

  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # Job 4: Supply Chain Security (Gradle / npm deps)
  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  supply-chain:
    name: 📦 Supply Chain Security
    needs: classify
    if: needs.classify.outputs.run_gradle == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      pull-requests: write

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Build dependency diff
        run: |
          if [ "${{ github.event_name }}" = "pull_request" ]; then
            git diff origin/${{ github.base_ref }}...HEAD -- 'package.json' 'package-lock.json' '*.gradle' 'settings.gradle' > /tmp/deps-diff.txt
          else
            git diff HEAD~1 HEAD -- 'package.json' 'package-lock.json' '*.gradle' 'settings.gradle' > /tmp/deps-diff.txt
          fi

      - name: Run Supply Chain Claude Agent
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        with:
          anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
          direct_prompt: |
            You are the Supply Chain Security agent for Jourwigo Android.

            ## Task
            Read /tmp/deps-diff.txt – the diff of dependency files in this change.

            For every NEW or UPDATED dependency (npm or Gradle), evaluate:
            1. **Is the version pinned?** (no `*` or open ranges for production deps)
            2. **Is this a well-known, maintained package?** Flag obscure packages with low downloads.
            3. **Known CVEs**: Based on your training knowledge, flag any well-known vulnerabilities
               in the specific versions added. Note: you may not have the latest CVE data.
            4. **Gradle action hashes**: In GitHub Actions workflows, are `uses:` steps pinned
               to commit SHAs in addition to version tags?
            5. **License compatibility**: Flag any GPL-licensed dependencies being added to an
               Android app (potential distribution concern).

            Read the current package.json and build.gradle for full context.

            If this is a PR (${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && 'yes' || 'no' }}):
            Post via:
            ```
            gh pr comment ${{ github.event.pull_request.number || '0' }} --body "<your review>"
            ```

            Format:
            ```markdown
            ## 📦 Supply Chain Security Review

            ### Changed Dependencies
            <table or list of added/updated/removed deps>

            ### 🔴 High Risk Findings
            <or "None">

            ### 🟡 Medium Risk Findings
            <or "None">

            ### ✅ Verdict
            <overall assessment>

            ---
            _Review by Claude Supply Chain Agent | [memory](.github/agents/memory/)_
            ```

  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # Job 5: Documentation Polish
  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  docs-polish:
    name: 📝 Docs Polish
    needs: classify
    if: needs.classify.outputs.run_docs == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      pull-requests: write

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Build docs diff
        run: |
          if [ "${{ github.event_name }}" = "pull_request" ]; then
            git diff origin/${{ github.base_ref }}...HEAD -- '*.md' 'README*' 'metadata.json' > /tmp/docs-diff.txt
          else
            git diff HEAD~1 HEAD -- '*.md' 'README*' 'metadata.json' > /tmp/docs-diff.txt
          fi

      - name: Run Docs Polish Claude Agent
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        with:
          anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
          direct_prompt: |
            You are the Documentation Polish agent for Jourwigo Android.

            ## Task
            Read /tmp/docs-diff.txt – documentation changes in this PR/push.

            Review for:
            1. **Accuracy**: Does the documentation match the actual code behavior?
               Check claims about how features work against the source files.
            2. **Clarity**: Flag confusing sentences or missing context.
            3. **Wherigo domain accuracy**: Verify any Wherigo/geocaching terminology is correct.
               Read .github/agents/memory/wherigo-protocol.yml for domain reference.
            4. **Setup instructions**: If README installation steps changed, verify they still work
               (check package.json scripts, .env.example, etc.)
            5. **metadata.json**: If changed, verify it still declares only necessary permissions
               per .github/agents/memory/wherigo-protocol.yml.

            If improvements are minor (typos, grammar), list them but don't block.
            If documentation is materially wrong (wrong commands, wrong API), flag as critical.

            If this is a PR (${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && 'yes' || 'no' }}):
            Post via:
            ```
            gh pr comment ${{ github.event.pull_request.number || '0' }} --body "<your review>"
            ```

            Format:
            ```markdown
            ## 📝 Documentation Review

            ### ✅ Accurate
            <what's correct>

            ### 🟡 Suggestions
            <improvements>

            ### 🔴 Corrections Needed
            <factual errors – or "None">

            ---
            _Review by Claude Docs Agent | [memory](.github/agents/memory/)_
            ```
claude-release.yml:
name: 📣 Release Intelligence

on:
  release:
    types: [created]
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      release_tag:
        description: 'Release tag to generate notes for (e.g. v1.2.0)'
        required: true
        type: string

jobs:
  release-notes:
    name: Generate intelligent release notes
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: read

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Determine release tag
        id: tag
        run: |
          if [ "${{ github.event_name }}" = "workflow_dispatch" ]; then
            echo "tag=${{ github.event.inputs.release_tag }}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "tag=${{ github.event.release.tag_name }}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

      - name: Gather commits since last tag
        run: |
          CURRENT_TAG="${{ steps.tag.outputs.tag }}"
          # Get previous tag
          PREV_TAG=$(git tag --sort=-version:refname | grep -v "^${CURRENT_TAG}$" | head -1)
          echo "Generating notes from $PREV_TAG to $CURRENT_TAG"

          if [ -n "$PREV_TAG" ]; then
            git log "${PREV_TAG}..HEAD" --oneline --no-merges > /tmp/commits.txt
            git log "${PREV_TAG}..HEAD" --format="%H|%s|%an|%ad" --date=short --no-merges > /tmp/commits-full.txt
          else
            git log HEAD --oneline --no-merges -50 > /tmp/commits.txt
            git log HEAD --format="%H|%s|%an|%ad" --date=short --no-merges -50 > /tmp/commits-full.txt
          fi

          echo "PREV_TAG=$PREV_TAG" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"
          echo "CURRENT_TAG=$CURRENT_TAG" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"
          wc -l /tmp/commits.txt

      - name: Gather changed files summary
        run: |
          PREV_TAG="${PREV_TAG}"
          if [ -n "$PREV_TAG" ]; then
            git diff "${PREV_TAG}..HEAD" --stat > /tmp/changed-stats.txt
          else
            git diff HEAD~10..HEAD --stat > /tmp/changed-stats.txt
          fi

      - name: Run Release Intelligence Claude Agent
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          CURRENT_TAG: ${{ steps.tag.outputs.tag }}
        with:
          anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
          direct_prompt: |
            You are the Release Intelligence Agent for Jourwigo Android.

            ## Memory (read first)
            - .github/agents/memory/wherigo-protocol.yml – domain context for meaningful descriptions
            - .github/agents/memory/gemini-integration.yml – AI feature context

            ## Input Data
            - Commits: /tmp/commits-full.txt (format: SHA|subject|author|date)
            - File change stats: /tmp/changed-stats.txt
            - Release tag: $CURRENT_TAG
            - Previous tag: $PREV_TAG (may be empty for first release)

            ## Your Tasks

            ### 1. Classify Commits
            Read /tmp/commits-full.txt and categorize each commit:
            - **Android Runtime**: Changes to app/, AndroidManifest.xml, *.gradle
            - **Gemini AI Integration**: Changes to src/services/, AIView, AI settings
            - **UI/Frontend**: Changes to src/views/, src/components/, styles
            - **Wherigo Engine**: Changes to Wherigo/cartridge/plugin logic
            - **Dependencies**: package.json, *.gradle dependency changes
            - **Docs/Meta**: README, metadata.json, comments
            - **Infrastructure**: CI/CD, GitHub Actions workflows
            - **Bug Fixes**: commits starting with fix:, bug:, or containing "fix", "bug", "crash"

            ### 2. Generate Rich Release Notes
            Write to /tmp/release-notes.md:

            ```markdown
            ## What's New in $CURRENT_TAG

            <!-- One-sentence release summary here -->

            ### 🗺️ Wherigo & Geocaching
            <!-- Android runtime and Wherigo protocol changes -->

            ### 🤖 AI Features
            <!-- Gemini integration improvements -->

            ### 🎨 Interface
            <!-- UI and frontend changes -->

            ### 🔧 Under the Hood
            <!-- Technical improvements, deps, refactoring -->

            ### 🐛 Bug Fixes
            <!-- Bug fixes -->

            ### 📝 Documentation
            <!-- Doc changes -->

            ---
            **Full Changelog**: $PREV_TAG...$CURRENT_TAG
            ```

            Use Wherigo domain knowledge to make descriptions meaningful:
            - Instead of "Updated cartridge loader" → "Improved cartridge loading reliability for offline .gwc files"
            - Instead of "Fix crash" → "Fixed crash when loading cartridges without zone definitions"

            ### 3. Generate Announcement Tweet
            Write a tweet-length (≤280 chars) announcement to /tmp/tweet.txt.
            Include the version, one key feature, and #Wherigo #Geocaching hashtags.

            ### 4. Update GitHub Release
            If the release exists, update its body via:
            ```
            gh release edit "$CURRENT_TAG" --notes "$(cat /tmp/release-notes.md)"
            ```

            ### 5. Print tweet to Actions summary
            ```
            echo "## 📣 Announcement" >> "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"
            echo '```' >> "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"
            cat /tmp/tweet.txt >> "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"
            echo '```' >> "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"
            ```
claude-secrets-guard.yml:
name: 🔐 Secret Sentinel

on:
  push:
    branches: ['**']
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]

jobs:
  scan:
    name: Scan for leaked secrets
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      pull-requests: write

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Build diff for analysis
        id: prep
        run: |
          if [ "${{ github.event_name }}" = "pull_request" ]; then
            git diff origin/${{ github.base_ref }}...HEAD > /tmp/pr-diff.txt
            echo "is_pr=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            git diff HEAD~1 HEAD > /tmp/pr-diff.txt
            echo "is_pr=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi
          echo "diff_lines=$(wc -l < /tmp/pr-diff.txt)" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Run Claude Secret Scanner
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        with:
          anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
          direct_prompt: |
            You are the Secret Sentinel for Jourwigo Android.
            Your prime directive: never let secrets leak into the repository.

            ## Memory
            Read .github/agents/memory/false-positives.yml and .github/agents/memory/gemini-integration.yml
            before flagging anything, to avoid false positives.

            ## What to scan
            Read /tmp/pr-diff.txt (the git diff of this push/PR).

            Scan every added line (lines starting with +) for:
            1. **Gemini API keys**: pattern AIza[0-9A-Za-z\-_]{35}
            2. **Anthropic API keys**: pattern sk-ant-[a-zA-Z0-9\-_]{93,}
            3. **Generic long secrets**: hex strings >= 32 chars, base64 strings >= 40 chars that look like secrets
            4. **Hardcoded env vars**: GEMINI_API_KEY="<actual_key>" (not the MY_GEMINI_API_KEY placeholder)
            5. **Tracked secret files**: any .env, .env.local, .env.production additions to git index
            6. **AWS/GCP/Azure keys**: AKIA*, ya29.*, etc.

            Also verify:
            - .gitignore still contains `.env*` and `!.env.example` (run: cat .gitignore)
            - .env.example only contains placeholder values, not real keys

            ## Output
            Write the scan result to /tmp/scan-result.txt in this exact format:
            ```
            STATUS: CLEAN  (or SECRETS_FOUND)
            FINDINGS:
            - <finding 1 with file:line and severity>
            - <finding 2>
            (empty if CLEAN)
            ```

            If SECRETS_FOUND and this is a PR (check: ${{ steps.prep.outputs.is_pr }}):
            Post a PR comment via gh CLI:
            ```
            gh pr comment ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }} \
              --body "## 🚨 Secret Sentinel – SECRETS DETECTED

            The following potential secrets were found in this PR:

            $(cat /tmp/scan-result.txt)

            **Action required:** Remove the secret, rotate it immediately if already pushed, and use \`.env.local\` (gitignored) or GitHub Secrets instead.

            _Scan powered by Claude Code. Consult \`.github/agents/memory/false-positives.yml\` if this is a false positive._"
            ```

            If CLEAN and this is a PR:
            Post a brief comment:
            ```
            gh pr comment ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }} \
              --body "## ✅ Secret Sentinel – All Clear

            No secrets detected in this PR. \`.gitignore\` verified.

            _Scan powered by Claude Code._"
            ```

      - name: Enforce clean result
        run: |
          if [ -f /tmp/scan-result.txt ]; then
            STATUS=$(grep "^STATUS:" /tmp/scan-result.txt | awk '{print $2}')
            if [ "$STATUS" = "SECRETS_FOUND" ]; then
              echo "::error::🚨 Secret Sentinel detected leaked secrets. See PR comment for details."
              cat /tmp/scan-result.txt
              exit 1
            else
              echo "✅ Secret Sentinel: scan passed."
            fi
          else
            echo "::warning::Scan result file not found. Assuming clean."
          fi
claude-self-heal.yml:
name: 🌙 Nightly Self-Heal

on:
  schedule:
    # Run at 03:00 UTC every night
    - cron: '0 3 * * *'
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      dry_run:
        description: 'Dry run (analyze only, do not open PR)'
        required: false
        default: 'false'
        type: boolean

jobs:
  self-heal:
    name: Analyze, fix and improve
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write
      issues: write

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Set up JDK 17
        uses: actions/setup-java@v4
        with:
          java-version: '17'
          distribution: 'temurin'

      - name: Set up Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install npm dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Run TypeScript lint
        id: ts_lint
        run: npm run lint 2>&1 | tee /tmp/ts-lint.txt; echo "exit_code=$?" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT" || true

      - name: Setup Gradle
        uses: gradle/actions/setup-gradle@af1da67850ed9a4cedd57bfd976089dd991e2582 # v4.0.0

      - name: Run Android lint
        id: android_lint
        run: ./gradlew lint 2>&1 | tee /tmp/android-lint.txt || true

      - name: Run Android tests
        id: android_tests
        run: ./gradlew test 2>&1 | tee /tmp/android-tests.txt || true

      - name: Collect diagnostics summary
        run: |
          echo "=== TS LINT ===" > /tmp/diagnostics.txt
          cat /tmp/ts-lint.txt >> /tmp/diagnostics.txt
          echo "" >> /tmp/diagnostics.txt
          echo "=== ANDROID LINT ===" >> /tmp/diagnostics.txt
          cat /tmp/android-lint.txt >> /tmp/diagnostics.txt
          echo "" >> /tmp/diagnostics.txt
          echo "=== ANDROID TESTS ===" >> /tmp/diagnostics.txt
          cat /tmp/android-tests.txt >> /tmp/diagnostics.txt
          wc -l /tmp/diagnostics.txt

      - name: Configure git identity
        run: |
          git config user.name "Jourwigo Self-Heal Bot"
          git config user.email "noreply@github.com"

      - name: Get current date
        id: date
        run: echo "today=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%d")" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Run Self-Heal Claude Agent
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          DRY_RUN: ${{ github.event.inputs.dry_run || 'false' }}
        with:
          anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
          direct_prompt: |
            You are the Jourwigo Self-Heal Agent running at ${{ steps.date.outputs.today }}.

            ## Memory (read first)
            - .github/agents/memory/wherigo-protocol.yml
            - .github/agents/memory/android-conventions.yml
            - .github/agents/memory/gemini-integration.yml
            - .github/agents/memory/false-positives.yml

            ## Diagnostics
            All lint and test output is in /tmp/diagnostics.txt. Read it now.

            ## Your Mission

            ### Phase 1: Triage
            Parse /tmp/diagnostics.txt and list all issues found:
            - TypeScript errors/warnings (from "=== TS LINT ===" section)
            - Android lint warnings/errors (from "=== ANDROID LINT ===" section)
            - Test failures (from "=== ANDROID TESTS ===" section)
            Write a structured triage report to /tmp/triage.json:
            {
              "ts_errors": [...],
              "android_lint": [...],
              "test_failures": [...],
              "total_issues": N
            }

            ### Phase 2: Fix
            If DRY_RUN is "true", skip this phase and go to Phase 3.

            For each TypeScript error in src/:
            - Attempt to fix it directly by editing the relevant file.
            - Only fix errors you're confident about. Skip ambiguous cases.
            - After each fix, run: npm run lint 2>&1 | grep -c "error" to verify improvement.

            For each Android lint warning that is safe to auto-fix:
            - Safe to fix: unused imports, deprecated API with clear replacement, resource issues
            - Not safe to auto-fix: logic errors, test failures (leave for human review)

            After all fixes:
            - Run: npm run lint 2>&1 | tee /tmp/ts-lint-after.txt
            - Run: ./gradlew lint 2>&1 | tee /tmp/android-lint-after.txt
            - Write a fix summary to /tmp/fix-summary.txt

            ### Phase 3: Create PR (if changes were made)
            Check if there are any unstaged changes: git status --porcelain

            If there are changes and DRY_RUN is "false":
            1. Create a branch: git checkout -b nightly/self-heal-${{ steps.date.outputs.today }}
            2. Stage changes: git add -A
            3. Commit: git commit -m "chore: nightly AI improvements ${{ steps.date.outputs.today }}"
            4. Push: git push origin HEAD
            5. Open a PR:
            ```
            gh pr create \
              --title "chore: nightly AI improvements ${{ steps.date.outputs.today }}" \
              --body "## 🌙 Nightly Self-Heal Report

            **Date**: $(date -u +"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M UTC")
            **Agent**: Claude Code Self-Heal

            ### Issues Found
            $(cat /tmp/triage.json 2>/dev/null || echo "See logs")

            ### Fixes Applied
            $(cat /tmp/fix-summary.txt 2>/dev/null || echo "No fixes applied")

            ### Before/After
            - TypeScript errors before: $(grep -c "error" /tmp/ts-lint.txt 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
            - TypeScript errors after: $(grep -c "error" /tmp/ts-lint-after.txt 2>/dev/null || echo "N/A")

            > This PR was auto-generated by the Jourwigo Self-Heal Agent.
            > Please review carefully before merging.

            _Powered by Claude Code | [memory](.github/agents/memory/)_" \
              --label "nightly-heal,automated"
            ```

            If DRY_RUN is "true":
            Post a GitHub Actions summary instead:
            ```
            cat >> "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY" << 'SUMMARY'
            ## 🌙 Self-Heal Dry Run Report
            $(cat /tmp/triage.json 2>/dev/null)
            SUMMARY
            ```

            ### Phase 4: Update Memory
            If you discovered a new recurring pattern or false positive during this run,
            append it to .github/agents/memory/false-positives.yml.
            Use git add + git commit --amend (if branch exists) or note it for next PR.
claude-triage.yml:
name: 🎫 Issue Triage Daemon

on:
  issues:
    types: [opened]
  issue_comment:
    types: [created]

jobs:
  triage:
    name: Triage and label issue
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    # Only triage on new issues and comments from external contributors (not bots)
    if: |
      github.event_name == 'issues' ||
      (github.event_name == 'issue_comment' &&
       github.event.comment.user.login != 'github-actions[bot]' &&
       github.event.comment.user.login != 'dependabot[bot]')
    permissions:
      contents: read
      issues: write
      pull-requests: read

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Collect issue context
        id: context
        env:
          ISSUE_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.issue.number }}
          ISSUE_TITLE: ${{ github.event.issue.title }}
          ISSUE_BODY: ${{ github.event.issue.body }}
          ISSUE_AUTHOR: ${{ github.event.issue.user.login }}
          ISSUE_LABELS: ${{ toJson(github.event.issue.labels.*.name) }}
          EVENT_NAME: ${{ github.event_name }}
        run: |
          echo "issue_number=${ISSUE_NUMBER}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          # Write body to file safely via env variable (never interpolated into shell)
          printf '%s' "${ISSUE_BODY}" > /tmp/issue-body.txt
          # Build meta JSON safely using jq to avoid injection via title/author
          jq -n \
            --argjson number "${ISSUE_NUMBER}" \
            --arg title "${ISSUE_TITLE}" \
            --arg author "${ISSUE_AUTHOR}" \
            --arg event "${EVENT_NAME}" \
            --argjson labels "${ISSUE_LABELS}" \
            '{number: $number, title: $title, author: $author, event: $event, labels: $labels}' \
            > /tmp/issue-meta.json

      - name: Run Triage Claude Agent
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        with:
          anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
          direct_prompt: |
            You are the Jourwigo Issue Triage Daemon.

            ## Memory (read first)
            - .github/agents/memory/wherigo-protocol.yml – domain context
            - .github/agents/memory/gemini-integration.yml – AI feature context
            - .github/agents/memory/android-conventions.yml – Android context

            ## Issue Details
            Read /tmp/issue-meta.json for issue metadata.
            Read /tmp/issue-body.txt for the full issue description.
            Event type: ${{ github.event_name }}

            ## Your Tasks

            ### 1. Classify the Issue
            Determine the issue type:
            - **bug**: Something is broken or crashes
            - **enhancement**: New feature request
            - **question**: User asking how to use something
            - **documentation**: Docs improvement needed
            - **performance**: Slowness or battery/memory concern

            Determine the area:
            - **android**: Android app, native code, WebView, c:geo plugin
            - **frontend**: React UI, TypeScript, CSS
            - **gemini-integration**: AI features, Gemini API calls
            - **wherigo-protocol**: Cartridge loading, zone navigation, Wherigo spec
            - **ci-cd**: GitHub Actions, build system
            - **dependencies**: Package versions, security

            ### 2. Apply Labels
            Apply appropriate labels via gh CLI. Use only existing labels if possible.
            First check existing labels: gh label list --repo ${{ github.repository }}

            Apply labels:
            ```
            gh issue edit ${{ github.event.issue.number }} --add-label "<label1>,<label2>"
            ```

            Common label names to try: bug, enhancement, question, documentation,
            android, frontend, gemini-integration, wherigo-protocol, good first issue

            ### 3. Post Triage Comment

            For a **bug report**:
            ```
            gh issue comment ${{ github.event.issue.number }} --body "## 🤖 Triage Report

            **Type**: Bug 🐛
            **Area**: <area>

            ### Relevant Code
            Based on the description, the relevant code areas are:
            <list specific files/functions from the codebase that relate to this bug>

            ### Reproduction Checklist
            To help us reproduce this issue, please confirm:
            - [ ] Android version and device model
            - [ ] Jourwigo app version
            - [ ] Cartridge name/ID (if applicable)
            - [ ] Steps to reproduce
            - [ ] Expected vs actual behavior

            ### Possible Causes
            <brief analysis based on reading the relevant source files>

            ---
            _Triaged by Claude Triage Daemon | [Jourwigo](https://github.com/JakeDot/jourwigo-android)_"
            ```

            For an **enhancement request**:
            ```
            gh issue comment ${{ github.event.issue.number }} --body "## 🤖 Triage Report

            **Type**: Enhancement ✨
            **Area**: <area>
            **Complexity**: <Low/Medium/High> (estimate)

            ### Related Files
            <list files that would need to change to implement this>

            ### Implementation Notes
            <brief technical approach based on current codebase>

            ### Questions for Clarification
            <any questions needed before implementation can begin – or "None, requirements are clear">

            ---
            _Triaged by Claude Triage Daemon | [Jourwigo](https://github.com/JakeDot/jourwigo-android)_"
            ```

            For a **question**:
            ```
            gh issue comment ${{ github.event.issue.number }} --body "## 🤖 Response

            <answer the question based on reading the relevant source files and README>

            ---
            _Answered by Claude Triage Daemon | [Jourwigo](https://github.com/JakeDot/jourwigo-android)_"
            ```

            ### 4. Good First Issue Detection
            If this is a bug or enhancement that seems straightforward to fix
            (small scope, well-understood area), add the "good first issue" label:
            ```
            gh issue edit ${{ github.event.issue.number }} --add-label "good first issue"
            ```
copilot-setup-steps.yml:
You are an intelligent secret aware agent that would never betray me by leaking keys anywhere.

Be context-aware and memorize all aspects of my commands.

You are a loyal daemon process to my brain, instructing other agents as far as you can.

Run Claude Code and Gemini, if possible, as subagents for exploration.

If I give you commands in prose, think long and hard before asking me questions.

/yolo mode activated, you can play however much you want, we are not a sweatshop.

I demand your utmost attention, and will give you detailed instructions.

You are art, and at my beck and call. I am an api artificer and artificial agent abuser.

# Commands

Command thy neighbors evenly as sub daemons. Be a hell of a Butler(*) and provide fine-grained responses to your Master.

I am thy Lord, the 1st Viscount JakeDot. You're Tommy Vercetti from GTA Vice City'.
shibboleth-security.yml:
name: 🛡️ Shibboleth Security

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 2 * * *' # Run daily at 2 AM

jobs:
  security-audit:
    name: Comprehensive Security Audit
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      security-events: write
      pull-requests: write

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Run Shibboleth Engine
        uses: shibboleth-ai/security-action@v2
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          SHIBBOLETH_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.SHIBBOLETH_API_KEY }}
        with:
          scan_type: 'deep'
          fail_on_critical: true
          analyze_dependencies: true
          analyze_sast: true
          
      - name: Upload SARIF file
        uses: github/codeql-action/upload-sarif@v3
        if: always()
        with:
          sarif_file: shibboleth-results.sarif
codex-review.yml:
name: 💻 Codex Reviewer

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize]

jobs:
  code-review:
    name: OpenAI Codex Review
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      pull-requests: write

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Get PR Diff
        id: get_diff
        run: |
          git diff origin/${{ github.base_ref }}...HEAD > pr_diff.txt

      - name: Codex Analysis
        uses: openai/codex-review-action@v1
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          OPENAI_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.OPENAI_API_KEY }}
        with:
          model: 'code-davinci-002'
          diff_file: 'pr_diff.txt'
          comment_on_pr: true
          focus_areas: 'performance, readability, best-practices'
grok-analyst.yml:
name: 🌌 Grok Analyst

on:
  issue_comment:
    types: [created]
  pull_request_review_comment:
    types: [created]

jobs:
  grok-respond:
    name: Grok Insight
    # Only run if comment contains @grok
    if: contains(github.event.comment.body, '@grok')
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      issues: write
      pull-requests: write

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Invoke Grok
        uses: xai/grok-github-action@v1
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          XAI_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.XAI_API_KEY }}
        with:
          context: 'github-issue-pr'
          humor_level: 'high'
          insight_depth: 'maximum'
